what_it_does: Provides a handoff brief from a Design DNA Pack build to a Visual Factory consumer, explaining the DNA model, current available token assets, a default dist schema proposal, and the decisions needed to compile the pack into the factory's ingest format.
observations: Frames the DNA Pack as an identity layer for compositional generation across visual and verbal output.
Defines layers: primitives, registries, templates, scales, dials, voice, skills/context, provenance, and invariants.
Includes an abridged tokens.json sample covering color, warmth anchors, type, space, radius, shadow, texture, motion, and dials.
Proposes a dist directory with manifest, DTCG-style tokens, registries, templates, scales, voice, invariants, skills, and optional build artifacts.
Lists default serialization, color, units, dial, template, invariant, delivery, versioning, and provenance choices.
Asks for decisions A through K, with emphasis on existing schema and composition grammar.
Offers reply modes: inline edits, a short lettered response, or a schema/sample composition.
standouts: The file explicitly prefers conforming to an existing factory schema over negotiating a new contract.
The dials are treated as a defining integration choice, especially warmth interpolation and register/density/modality delivery.
The template layer is described as still being shaped while the primitive token layer is presented as sampled and locked.
Invariants are intended for both machine checking and LLM guidance.
conventions: Uses a handoff-letter format with sender, receiver, repo paths, purpose, numbered sections, and lettered decision prompts.
Separates current facts, default proposal, and requested decisions.
Uses concrete file-tree and JSON examples to describe the intended artifact shape.
Uses aliases, DTCG terminology, and role-to-token mapping language consistently.
Keeps the tone collaborative and contract-oriented rather than implementation-complete.
concerns: The file is intentionally a negotiation document, so several target formats remain unresolved until the Visual Factory replies.
The embedded edit-back path in section 5 differs from the supplied file path by omitting docs/letters.
The template/composition grammar is described as the hardest part to guess and is not settled in this file.
notes: If briefing someone on this file: it is the contract-negotiation handoff asking the Visual Factory how it wants the Design DNA Pack compiled.
questions: Does the Visual Factory already have a token, theme, component, or layout schema to target?
What composition grammar should templates be expressed in?
Should dials be delivered as interpolation specs, precomputed sets, both, or anchors only?
What format should machine-checkable invariants use?
Should runtime consumption be static files or a queryable interface?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it explains the DNA Pack, proposes a default output shape, and asks for specific integration decisions.
apparent_themes: separation of identity from product content
intention_signals: It seems intended to prevent the producer from inventing an incompatible grammar by asking the consuming factory to provide or approve the ingest contract before compilation.
novelty: The file combines design-token packaging, verbal identity, parametric dials, template composition, provenance, invariants, and AI skill routing into one proposed handoff contract.
contribution: Contributes a neutral integration brief and proposed artifact ledger for carrying identity primitives, role mappings, composition templates, dials, voice rules, invariants, provenance, and context-loading instructions into a fused generation system.
summary_for_embedding: A handoff markdown file that explains a Design DNA Pack, shows an abridged token sample, proposes a dist schema, and asks the Visual Factory for schema, dial, template, invariant, voice, runtime, and versioning decisions.
intention_for_embedding: This was for aligning the Design DNA Pack producer with the Visual Factory consumer before compiling identity tokens, templates, voice, invariants, and skills into ingestible artifacts.
